title: Cheviot Hills Memorial commemorated Memorial
memorial_type: Cheviot Memorial - commemorates all lives lost in aircraft crashes across the Cheviot Hills during WW2

maintainer.notes: Memorial commemorates all lives lost in aircraft crashes across the Cheviot Hills during WW2.

external_narratives.summary: Official National Park page for the Cheviot Airmen Memorial. Commemorates 58 airmen killed in 19 separate crashes in the Cheviot Hills during WW2. Originally dedicated by the Duke of Gloucester on 19 May 1995; upgraded granite memorial rededicated on 6 September 2018 for RAF centenary.
key_details: Original memorial 19 May 1995 by Duke of Gloucester
Located in College Valley opposite Cuddystone Hall

external_narratives.summary: Imperial War Museums register entry for the Allied Airmen memorial in the Cheviot Hills, Northumberland. Documents the collective memorial to all airmen lost in the hills during WW2.

Feature article on the Cheviot Hills airmen memorial and the history of wartime crashes in the area. Cloud cover over the hills was a major contributing factor to the 19 crashes between 1939 and 1946.
key_details: Cloud cover identified as major crash cause

external_narratives.summary: Local guide to WW2 plane crash sites in the Cheviot Hills near Hethpool. Documents multiple crash locations accessible from the College Valley area.
key_details: Walking routes to crash sites from Hethpool

sources: Current coords 55.375 -1.902 fall on coastal plain near Alnwick — not in hills
location_confidence.verification_notes: INACCURATE. Current coords (55.375, -1.902) fall at longitude of coastal plain near Alnwick, not in the Cheviot Hills. Summit area at ~55.478, -2.148 is a better approximation for a general Cheviot memorial. Confidence approximate — memorial may be at a specific col or hill not yet identified.
